# 🇱🇰 Sri Lanka This Week

![LastUpdated](https://img.shields.io/badge/last_updated-2026--04--15_10:16:28-green)
[![RSS](https://img.shields.io/badge/RSS-Feed-orange)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/refs/heads/main/rss.xml)

*Generated by gpt-5 from 98 English News Articles published between 2026-04-08 and 2026-04-14.*

## 1. Sri Lanka and the IMF reached a staff-level agreement on the fifth and sixth reviews, unlocking about $700m subject to Board approval.

The deal lifted cumulative disbursements under the four-year facility to roughly $2.4bn once cleared, compared with zero at the programme’s start in 2023, and followed a 15‑day mission in the capital. The arrangement kept reforms on track as price pressures eased to low single digits early in 2026 and official reserves climbed to their strongest level in years.

## 2. Colombo bourse rallied, with the ASPI up 4.21% and the S&P SL20 up 4.42% in a session marked by heavy bank crossings.

Turnover reached Rs 6.61bn with 11 negotiated blocks, led by trades in lenders and conglomerates that accounted for a large share of value. The rupee firmed to Rs 315.10/25 per dollar in the spot market while government bond yields fell, reversing earlier risk aversion.

## 3. Government launched a Rs 2.16bn upgrade of Colombo Fort railway station financed with Asian Development Bank support.

Works were scheduled to finish within 15 months, adding automated ticketing, lifts and new pedestrian links to improve access for elderly and disabled riders. The hub handled hundreds of thousands of daily passengers, making enhancements material to network efficiency.

## 4. Train services on the Northern Line resumed after India-funded repairs to cyclone-damaged sections.

The grant element totalled $5m within a wider $450m package and completion came ahead of the Sinhala and Tamil New Year. Restored links reconnected areas affected by Ditwah, shortening journeys relative to post-storm diversions.

## 5. Asian Development Outlook 2026 projected growth of 4.0% next year and 4.2% in 2027 for Sri Lanka.

The flagship publication recorded a firm 5.0% expansion in 2025 alongside a third straight current‑account surplus and multi‑year‑high reserves. It also placed consumer‑price gains at 5.2% in 2026, higher than the low single‑digit readings seen early this year, with energy costs and trade disruptions cited as risks.

## 6. Regulators tightened constraints at NDB after a reported Rs 13.2bn internal fraud came to light in Parliament.

Measures set out included suspending cash dividends due in April, curbing discretionary outlays and commissioning an independent forensic review, while the impact was estimated at about 0.7% of unaudited assets. Lawmakers pressed oversight bodies on why controls and supervision failed to detect irregularities sooner.

## 7. Commercial Bank partnered with NCG Express to roll out fully digital bus ticketing across selected routes.

Conductors began using integrated devices that accept cards, QR payments and mobile wallets, with instant settlement through the acquiring network. The operator planned to scale to its full fleet, offering cashless options to thousands of daily commuters under the national ticketing initiative.

## 8. Colombo Dockyard named a new non-executive chairman and added directors after Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders took a 51% stake.

The leadership changes kept the incumbent managing director in post and brought prominent Sri Lankan executives onto the board to strengthen governance. The new chair, a veteran naval architect, previously held senior design roles at major subcontinental yards, signalling an emphasis on engineering depth.

## 9. Motor Traffic Commissioner General Kamal Amarasinghe was further remanded until 20 April by a Kurunegala magistrate.

The arrest on 27 March stemmed from an investigation into alleged irregular vehicle registration, including an altered cab chassis number. Proceedings continued as the Criminal Investigation Department pursued charges of aiding and abetting.

## 10. International Solar Alliance concluded a four-day mission in Colombo to operationalise a country partnership strategy exceeding 4 GW of projects.

The visit prioritised bankable execution, with floating photovoltaics, battery storage and a technology resource centre at a leading engineering faculty among focal points. Officials framed the pipeline as a path to trim fuel imports and lift clean‑power penetration beyond the roughly 1 GW of installed solar already in place.
